pi_items.source: Task description, setup_task.sh, export_result.sh history queries, verifier.py domain checks. CDC FluView (https://www.cdc.gov/fluview/), CDC MMWR (https://www.cdc.gov/mmwr/), WHO Disease Outbreak News (https://www.who.int/emergencies/disease-outbreak-news), CDC NNDSS (https://www.cdc.gov/nndss/) are all real public endpoints.
setup_task.sh line 49 and export_result.sh line 50: SQL query checks for moz_bookmarks WHERE title='Disease Surveillance'
setup_task.sh line 49 and export_result.sh line 69 both reference this exact path
task.json description states: 'You are a state epidemiologist preparing a weekly infectious disease briefing'

privileged_info_summary: The task is a disease surveillance briefing compilation task that requires accessing real public health data from CDC and WHO. The agent must visit cdc.gov and who.int domains, specifically: CDC FluView for weekly influenza surveillance, CDC MMWR for recent reports, WHO Disease Outbreak News for global outbreaks, and CDC NNDSS for nationally notifiable diseases. All data sources are real government health surveillance systems. The agent must create a Firefox bookmark folder named 'Disease Surveillance' containing at least 5 bookmarks and output a JSON report to /home/ga/Documents/surveillance_briefing.json. The role is a state epidemiologist conducting public health research.
